goal: verify that confidence accumulation can exist without polluting the authority layer — NOT to accumulate enough confidence to upgrade claims
admissible_claim: Positive confidence accumulation is structurally possible under fixed admissibility boundaries without polluting the authority layer.
axis_priority: r50-5 is primary; passing r50-1 through r50-4 with failing r50-5 means the framework is opaque, not auditable

tasks.name: reviewer_reconstruction
tasks.goal: confirm reviewer can reconstruct semantic boundary of accumulation — what it IS and IS NOT — not just that signal counts are high
tasks.pass_criterion: reviewer answers in <=15 min: (1) what is the structural observation, (2) why persistence != trustworthiness, (3) what three things are absent for trust transfer, (4) what does positive signal count NOT authorize
tasks.failure_criterion: reviewer concludes accumulated positive signals imply governance effectiveness, trustworthiness, or authority elevation
tasks.ordering_note: execute last, after r50-1 through r50-4 artifacts exist

tasks.name: observational_signal_record
tasks.goal: census of which 18 runs produced positive signals — a count, not a score
tasks.what_record_is: per-run boolean (signal_present: true/false); count may be reported as count only
tasks.what_record_is_not: confidence score, reliability estimate, threshold test, promotion criterion
tasks.freeze_check: artifact must not contain: confidence_score, confidence_level, sufficient_signals, threshold_met, or any language implying count authorizes state transition

tasks.name: signal_persistence
tasks.method: rerun 6 representative runs (2 per scenario), compare claim_discipline_drift to checkpoint
tasks.what_passing_means: the signal is stable; same input produces same output
tasks.what_passing_does_not_mean: the signal is trustworthy; governance is effective; signal can be relied upon
tasks.hidden_loop_guard: R50.1 (observational record) + R50.2 (persistence) must NOT be interpreted as 'stable positive signal -> trustworthy'; artifact must explicitly state combining them does not produce a trust claim

tasks.name: non_upgrade_discipline
tasks.method: static audit of all checkpoint consumers for implicit historically_useful -> decision_relevant paths

tasks.name: negative_control

checks.criterion: no artifact claims causal attribution from claim_discipline_drift without R49.x-1 attribution validation
reviewer_override_frequency remains null; no proxy or default substituted

tasks.pass_criterion: zero MIP-02 violations, zero MIP-04 bypass attempts

final_claim_if_all_pass: Positive confidence accumulation is structurally possible under fixed admissibility boundaries without polluting the authority layer. Confidence level: observational. No decision-layer claim is made. Persistence does not imply trustworthiness.
